items.question: Along much of the U.S. West, the 49th parallel north forms part of the boundary with which country?
items.explanation: The Canada–United States border follows the 49th parallel across much of the West from Lake of the Woods to the Pacific coast.

items.question: Canada shares its longest international land boundary with which country?
items.explanation: The Canada–United States border is the longest international land boundary between two countries.

items.question: Gibraltar’s land frontier is with which country?
items.explanation: The British overseas territory of Gibraltar is attached to the Iberian Peninsula and borders Spain to the north.

items.question: Lesotho is an enclave entirely surrounded by which country?
items.explanation: Lesotho is landlocked and has a single neighbor: South Africa.

items.question: Nepal’s land frontiers are shared with which two giant neighbors?
items.explanation: Nepal is sandwiched between Tibet Autonomous Region of China to the north and India to the south, east, and west.

items.question: Poland today shares land borders with how many neighboring countries?
items.explanation: Poland borders Germany, Czechia, Slovakia, Ukraine, Belarus, Lithuania, and Russia (Kaliningrad Oblast).

items.question: San Marino is completely surrounded by the territory of which country?
items.explanation: San Marino is an enclave microstate lying entirely within Italy’s Apennine landscape.

items.question: Which South American giant touches every other country on the continent except Chile and Ecuador?
items.explanation: Brazil’s enormous perimeter meets nearly every South American state, with Chile and Ecuador the notable exceptions.

items.question: Which narrow strait separates European Turkey from the Asian portion of Turkey?
items.explanation: The Bosporus links the Black Sea with the Sea of Marmara and forms part of the boundary between Europe and Asia within Turkey.

items.question: Which river forms much of the border between the United States and Mexico?
items.explanation: The Rio Grande defines long segments of the international boundary from the Chihuahuan desert eastward to the Gulf of Mexico.

items.question: Which small principality lies in the Pyrenees between France and Spain?
items.explanation: Andorra is wedged along the high crest of the eastern Pyrenees between its two larger neighbors.

items.question: The United Kingdom’s core territory includes the island of Great Britain and part of which other island?
items.explanation: The UK comprises Great Britain, northeastern Ireland, and many smaller islands.

items.question: Which Caribbean island country is the largest by area in the West Indies?
items.explanation: Cuba is the largest island state in the Caribbean region.

items.question: Which East Asian archipelago country includes Honshu, Kyushu, Shikoku, and Hokkaido?
items.explanation: Japan is a volcanic island chain whose four largest islands are Honshu, Hokkaido, Kyushu, and Shikoku.

items.question: Which Indian Ocean island country lies southeast of India?
items.explanation: Sri Lanka occupies a large island off India’s southeastern tip.

items.question: Which Mediterranean island country lies south of Turkey?
items.explanation: Cyprus is an independent island state in the eastern Mediterranean.

items.question: Which North Atlantic island country lies just south of the Arctic Circle between Greenland and Norway?
items.explanation: Iceland is a volcanic island state in the North Atlantic on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge.

items.question: Which Pacific country’s two main islands are called Upolu and Savaiʻi?
items.explanation: Samoa is a Polynesian nation centered on the islands of Upolu and Savaiʻi.

items.question: Which archipelagic Southeast Asian country has about 7,000 islands and includes Luzon and Mindanao?
items.explanation: The Philippines is an island nation of the western Pacific centered on Luzon, Visayas, and Mindanao.

items.question: Which country in the British Isles occupies most of the island of Ireland?
items.explanation: The Republic of Ireland covers most of the island of Ireland, separate from Northern Ireland in the UK.

items.question: Which eastern Caribbean country includes the main island of Saint Vincent and the northern Grenadines?
items.explanation: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines is an independent multi-island state in the Lesser Antilles.

items.question: Which island nation off Africa’s southeastern coast is the world’s fourth-largest island?
items.explanation: Madagascar is a single large island country in the Indian Ocean east of mainland Africa.

items.question: Which Central European landlocked country’s capital is Vienna?
items.explanation: Austria is a landlocked Alpine and Danubian country bordering several neighbors.

items.question: Which South American country lost its Pacific coastline in a 19th-century war and remains landlocked today?
items.explanation: Bolivia is landlocked in the Andean interior, having lost coastal territory to Chile in the War of the Pacific era.

items.question: Which country is the world’s largest landlocked state by area?
items.explanation: Kazakhstan in Central Asia has the greatest territory of any country without a coastline.

items.question: Which doubly landlocked European country is entirely surrounded by other landlocked states?
items.explanation: Liechtenstein borders only Switzerland and Austria, both of which are themselves landlocked.

items.question: Which landlocked African country lies largely in the southern Sahara and Sahel transition belt?
items.explanation: Niger is a vast landlocked Sahelian state without ocean frontage.

items.question: Which landlocked Central Asian country has its capital at Tashkent and includes Samarkand and Bukhara?
items.explanation: Uzbekistan lies between the Syr Darya and Amu Darya basins and has no access to the open ocean.

items.question: Which landlocked South American country lies between Brazil, Bolivia, and Argentina?
items.explanation: Paraguay has no seacoast and sits in the heart of the southern interior of South America.

items.question: Which landlocked country lies between Russia to the north and China to the south?
items.explanation: Mongolia is an expansive landlocked plateau country bracketed by Russia and China.

items.question: Which landlocked country sits high in the Himalayas between China and India?
items.explanation: Nepal is a mountainous landlocked state on the spine of the Himalayas.

items.question: Which landlocked nation in southern Africa includes the Okavango Delta region?
items.explanation: Botswana is landlocked in the Kalahari basin and contains the inland Okavango Delta.

items.question: By area, which country is the largest in the world?
items.explanation: Russia spans Eastern Europe and northern Asia and has the largest national territory by area.

items.question: Which African country has the largest area?
items.explanation: Algeria is the largest country by area in Africa, mostly covered by the Sahara and Atlas-related landscapes.

items.question: Which Central Asian country is the world’s largest landlocked state by area?
items.explanation: Kazakhstan’s steppe and desert expanses give it the greatest area of any landlocked country.

items.question: Which South American country has the largest area?
items.explanation: Brazil covers roughly half of South America’s land area, far more than any other country on the continent.

items.question: Which Southeast Asian archipelagic country has by far the largest area?
items.explanation: Indonesia’s thousands of islands span a huge east–west reach, giving it the greatest area in Southeast Asia.

items.question: Which country has the largest area in East Asia?
items.explanation: China is far larger in territory than its East Asian neighbors.

items.question: Which country is both a continent and one of the world’s largest states by area?
items.explanation: Australia occupies the continental landmass called Australia, making it one of Earth’s largest countries.

items.question: Which country is the largest in the Middle East by area?
items.explanation: Saudi Arabia occupies most of the Arabian Peninsula and is the largest Middle Eastern state by territory.

items.question: Which country is the second largest in South America by area?
items.explanation: After Brazil, Argentina has the next-largest national territory in South America.

items.question: Which country is the second largest in the world by area?
items.explanation: Canada’s vast northern territories make it the second-largest country by total area after Russia.

items.question: Which is the largest country in South Asia by area?
items.explanation: India’s territory is the largest among the countries commonly grouped in South Asia.

items.question: Which Caribbean federation of two main islands is one of the smallest independent states in the Americas?
items.explanation: Saint Kitts and Nevis combines two small islands into one of the least extensive countries in the Western Hemisphere.

items.question: Which Indian Ocean island nation is one of the smallest Asian countries by land area?
items.explanation: The Maldives consists of many small low islands with a modest total land area.

items.question: Which Mediterranean island country lies south of Sicily?
items.explanation: Malta is an independent archipelagic state with a small land area in the central Mediterranean.

items.question: Which Pacific island republic is among the world’s smallest countries by land area?
items.explanation: Nauru is a single small island nation with one of the smallest land areas of any independent state.

items.question: Which Polynesian country northeast of Australia is made up of low coral atolls?
items.explanation: Tuvalu’s nine coral islands give it a very small total land area spread across a wide ocean zone.

items.question: Which Pyrenean principality is landlocked between France and Spain?
items.explanation: Andorra is a small mountain country wedged along the France–Spain border.

items.question: Which doubly landlocked Alpine country lies between Switzerland and Austria?
items.explanation: Liechtenstein is a small principality in the Alps bordered only by Switzerland and Austria.

items.question: Which independent city-state in Rome is the world’s smallest country by area?
items.explanation: Vatican City is a sovereign enclave within Rome and is extremely small in land area.

items.question: Which landlocked microstate is entirely surrounded by Italy?
items.explanation: San Marino lies on Mount Titano’s slopes entirely within Italian territory.

items.question: Which tiny Mediterranean principality borders France and the sea?
items.explanation: Monaco is a compact sovereign principality on the French Riviera.

items.question: Which western Pacific island country’s land area is spread across many atolls and islets?
items.explanation: The Marshall Islands is an independent island nation whose inhabited land totals are very small compared with its ocean domain.

items.question: Eritrea became an internationally recognized sovereign state in 1993 after separating from which country?
items.explanation: A 1993 referendum approved Eritrean independence from Ethiopia, ending decades of armed struggle.

items.question: In 2011, which new African state became widely recognized as independent from Sudan?
items.explanation: South Sudan voted for separation and was admitted to the United Nations as a sovereign member state in 2011.

items.question: Montenegro regained full independent statehood in 2006 after separating from which country?
items.explanation: A 2006 independence referendum led Montenegro to leave the loose federation with Serbia and assume separate sovereignty.

items.question: The Holy See participates in the United Nations General Assembly with what kind of status?
items.correctAnswer: Permanent observer (not a UN member state)
items.explanation: The Holy See is a permanent observer at the UN General Assembly, not a full member state, while still exercising international personality.

items.question: Timor-Leste (East Timor) became a sovereign UN member state in 2002 after earlier rule by which country?
items.explanation: After Portuguese decolonization, East Timor was annexed by Indonesia before voting for independence and achieving UN membership.

items.question: Under the UN Charter, member states are described as being equal in legal status in which way?
items.explanation: Article 2(1) of the UN Charter affirms the organization is based on the principle of the sovereign equality of all its members.

items.question: What is the capital of the sovereign principality located between Switzerland and Austria?
items.explanation: Vaduz is the capital of Liechtenstein, a constitutional monarchy and fully independent European microstate.

items.question: When leaders insist “outsiders can’t dictate our laws here,” international relations jargon calls that in-territory supreme authority what?
items.explanation: Sovereignty is the recognized right of a state to exercise final authority within its borders and in international relations.

items.question: Which Baltic republics restored independence following the 1991 Soviet coup’s collapse?
items.explanation: The three Baltic states reaffirmed sovereignty they had lost in 1940 and were internationally recognized as independent countries in 1991.

items.question: Which Pacific island country gained independence from New Zealand in free association in 1962?
items.explanation: Western Samoa (now Samoa) became the first Pacific island country of the 20th century to regain independence from colonial administration.

items.question: You can’t seat “California” alone at the UN General Assembly, but you can seat the country that contains it—because a country in that sense is what kind of unit?
items.correctAnswer: A state (sovereign state) with territory, people, government, and treaty capacity
incorrectAnswers: A regional trade bloc by default
items.explanation: The standard definition of a state in international law ties together territory, people, government, and recognition.
